decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack to cheat powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn into play early in the game. This strategy can overwhelm the opponent and secure a quick victory. The deck also includes counter spells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce to protect its combo pieces. Additionally, cards like Brainstorm and Ponder help to filter through the deck and find key cards quickly. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently and win the game before the opponent can respond effectively.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the synergy between lands and control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building a powerful board state. By using cards like Crop Rotation, Thespian's Stage, Dark Depths, and Life from the Loam, the deck can quickly assemble powerful land combinations. Additionally, cards like Mox Diamond, Wasteland, and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale provide disruption and protection against opposing threats. This deck aims to control the game through land manipulation and resource denial, ultimately overwhelming the opponent with its powerful land-based combos.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through the use of artifacts, preventing opponents from playing spells or utilizing their resources effectively. By setting up a "prison" of powerful artifacts like Trinisphere and Planar Nexus, the deck aims to lock down the opponent's options while also ramping into powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. Utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator and The One Ring allows for additional utility and versatility in managing the board state.

The deck focuses on creating a lock-down prison using artifacts to control and disrupt the opponent's resources. Using powerful artifacts like Karn, the Great Creator and Trinisphere, along with mana acceleration from Urza lands and Grim Monolith, the goal is to limit the opponent's options while setting up for a big play with cards like The One Ring or Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. The deck aims to create a powerful board state that overwhelms the opponent and prevents them from mounting a comeback.

The deck focuses on generating a high storm count to cast Tendrils of Agony for a lethal amount of damage. It utilizes cards like Dark Ritual, Lion's Eye Diamond, Lotus Petal, and Chrome Mox to quickly generate mana and cast multiple spells in a single turn. Burning Wish allows for access to key combo pieces from the sideboard, while Echo of Eons and Brainstorm provide card draw and manipulation. The inclusion of cards like Defense Grid and Gaea's Will help protect the combo and ensure a successful storm turn.

This deck focuses on utilizing Nic Fit strategy to control the game by sacrificing creatures to gain advantage and disrupt opponent's plans. By using cards like Cabal Therapy and Veteran Explorer to generate mana, the deck can efficiently cast powerful threats like Grist, the Hunger Tide and Pernicious Deed to control the board. With a mix of removal spells like Assassin's Trophy and Witherbloom Charm, along with impactful creatures like Alpha Deathclaw and Endurance, this deck aims to outvalue the opponent and win the game through attrition and resource management.

The primary strategy of this deck is to establish a lock on the game using powerful artifacts and planeswalkers to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By ramping into large threats like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, while also utilizing powerful artifacts like Trinisphere and The One Ring, this deck aims to prevent the opponent from executing their strategy effectively. With a focus on artifact synergy and mana acceleration, this deck aims to establish dominance and control over the game.

The main focus of this UWx Control deck is to utilize a high number of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage engines to maintain control of the game and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With powerful planeswalkers like Jace, the Mind Sculptor and Narset, Parter of Veils, alongside efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind out incremental advantages until it can close out the game. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value and flexibility in longer games.

The deck focuses on leveraging artifacts to quickly overwhelm opponents. By utilizing powerful artifact creatures like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Patchwork Automaton, as well as artifact mana sources like Mox Opal and Lotus Petal, the deck aims to establish an aggressive board presence early on. This is further supported by disruptive spells like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor, along with utility cards like Mishra's Bauble and Pithing Needle. The inclusion of Urza's Saga and Tormod's Crypt also provide additional versatility and graveyard hate, making this deck a formidable force in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck aims to control the game by utilizing lands as a resource, with cards like Dark Depths and Urza's Saga for powerful win conditions. It also includes disruption cards like Wasteland and Boseiju, Who Endures to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck focuses on generating advantage through cards like Life from the Loam and Exploration, while also having ways to protect itself with cards like Maze of Ith and Karakas. Overall, the strategy revolves around using lands efficiently to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent.

This deck focuses on utilizing lands as a control strategy, with cards like Thespian's Stage and Dark Depths to create an indestructible 20/20 creature. The key concept revolves around the synergy between Life from the Loam to recurring lands, Exploration to ramp up, and Crop Rotation to search for specific lands. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Wasteland and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale to slow down opponents. By constantly controlling the board through land interactions and generating value from lands, this deck aims to outlast opponents and secure victory.

The deck revolves around assembling a powerful combo using Doomsday to manipulate the top cards of the deck and then win the game with Thassa's Oracle or other means. It includes protection spells like Force of Will and Daze to ensure the combo goes off without disruption. The mana base is designed to support the combo with efficient fetch lands and dual lands. The deck also includes card selection and filtering tools like Ponder, Brainstorm, and Thoughtseize to find the pieces needed to execute the combo efficiently.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's plays through counterspells, removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, and the lockout effect of Standstill. It utilizes a mix of creatures like Timeless Dragon and planeswalkers like Jace, the Mind Sculptor and Teferi, Time Raveler for additional control options. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Wasteland and Mystic Sanctuary to disrupt the opponent's mana base. The goal is to control the game tempo and win through incremental advantage while preventing the opponent from executing their game plan effectively.

The deck focuses on locking down the opponent with artifacts that disrupt their resources and slow down their game plan. Cards like Trinisphere and Urza's Tower limit the opponent's options, while cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storm provide additional control and board presence. The deck also has powerful win conditions like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and The One Ring to close out the game. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through artifact manipulation and disruption, leading to a strong advantage and eventual victory.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive threats like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Daze and Force of Will. It aims to control the board with efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Murktide Regent, while also utilizing cards like Mishra's Bauble and Brainstorm to generate card advantage and dig for key pieces. The deck's tempo-oriented strategy allows it to quickly close out games by applying pressure and disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on casting powerful creatures from the graveyard with cards like Violent Outburst and Living End, while also utilizing cards like Architects of Will and Curator of Mysteries to manipulate the top of the deck. By cycling through cards with Street Wraith and Shardless Agent, the player can fill the graveyard quickly to set up explosive plays. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect the combo. Overall, the strategy revolves around quickly assembling a board presence from the graveyard to overwhelm the opponent.
